nodes.context: Large Russian gang based in Saint Petersburg, involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, money laundering, and other crimes.
Leading criminal group in Leningrad, rival of Tambovskaya Bratva, involved in narcotics trade.
Criminal group from Tatarstan, fought Tambovskaya Bratva for control of St. Petersburg's energy trade.
Chechen organized crime group, mentioned as having a member who introduced Russian mafia to Japanese yakuza.
Russian criminal group, rival of Tambovskaya Bratva, involved in similar activities.
Russian criminal fund or association, listed as an ally of Tambovskaya Bratva.
Criminal group ousted from narcotics trade by Alexander Malyshev in 1993.
Criminal group suspected by Kumarin of attempting to kill him in 1994.

edges.context: The two gangs clashed in a bloody armed conflict in 1989 for control of the Leningrad sea port and narcotics trafficking.
Kumarin briefly allied with Alexander Malyshev to fight the Kazan gang after Malyshev ousted the Azerbaijani mafia.
The Kazan Gang fought the Tambov Gang for control of St. Petersburg's energy trade and seaport in the mid-1990s.
A Chechen mafia member introduced the Russian mafia (including Petrov, Kuzmin, Malyshev) to a Japanese yakuza.
The Solntsevskaya Bratva is listed as a rival of the Tambovskaya Bratva.
Obshchak is listed as an ally of the Tambovskaya Bratva.
Alexander Malyshev ousted the Azerbaijani mafia from the narcotics trade before allying with Kumarin.
Kumarin believed the Velikie Luki were responsible for a murder attempt on him in 1994.
